lastTicket.title: yah-ui P3: Architecture live wiring + env-adapter hooks

ticket.title: Wire Board tab to live env adapter: listTickets/listRelays/getTicket + WorkItem types + replace mockTickets
next_steps: Add WorkItem/WorkItemAnno/TicketStatus/WorkItemAnchor/ListTicketsResult/ListRelaysResult/GetTicketResult types to yah-ui/src/env/types.ts
Extend Rpc interface in yah-ui/src/env/index.ts with listTickets/listRelays/getTicket
Wire invoke('arch_list_tickets'|'arch_list_relays'|'arch_get_ticket') in yah-ui/src/env/tauri.ts
Browser stub returns empty arrays in yah-ui/src/env/browser.ts
Add workItemToTicket() mapper (RPC WorkItem -> UI Ticket) — anchors[0] sources file/line; flatten anno.* fields
App.tsx: replace useState(mockTickets) with effect that getEnv() + listTickets + listRelays, plus arch:event index_finished re-fetch
gotchas: App.tsx still falls back to mockTickets when env is browser OR no active rig — keep dev-without-Tauri working
assumes: R024-T2 will land arch_attach_rig/arch_set_active_rig commands so the user can pick a rig from the UI; until then YAH_RIG_ROOT is the bootstrap path

before: R024-T2 will land arch_attach_rig/arch_set_active_rig commands so the user can pick a rig from the UI; until then YAH_RIG_ROOT is the bootstrap path
after: R024-T2 will land arch_attach_rig/arch_set_active_rig commands so the user can pick a rig from the UI; until then YAH_RIG_ROOT is the bootstrap path

Tauri command names follow snake_case mapping from #[tauri::command] fn names: arch_list_tickets / arch_list_relays / arch_get_ticket — confirmed against app/tauri/src/lib.rs:88-90 invoke_handler list
serve.ts Bun-globals errors are pre-existing — handled under R015-T6

before: App.tsx still falls back to mockTickets when env is browser OR no active rig — keep dev-without-Tauri working
after: App.tsx still falls back to mockTickets when env is browser OR no active rig — keep dev-without-Tauri working
childCounts on zone cards is currently undefined — TitleBar/Board don't render the smell badge for live data until inferred-epic surfacing lands

Wire-up complete and bundles clean. Wire types added to env/types.ts (WireWorkItem/WireWorkItemAnno/WireWorkItemAnchor/WireTicketStatus/WireWorkItemType + ListTickets/ListRelays/GetTicket params+results). Rpc interface in env/index.ts extended with listTickets/listRelays/getTicket; env/tauri.ts wires invoke('arch_list_tickets' | 'arch_list_relays' | 'arch_get_ticket'); env/browser.ts returns empty stubs. New env/mapper.ts has workItemToTicket() — sources file/line from anchors[0], narrows anno.kind to TicketKind, defaults missing status to 'open', sets isZone only on explicit kind('epic'). App.tsx now: keeps mockTickets as initial state (UX during boot / no-rig), useEffect calls getEnv→listTickets+listRelays→workItemToTicket on mount, replaces tickets on first non-empty fetch, subscribes to arch:event { event: 'index_finished' } and re-fetches. bun run typecheck: 0 errors in modified files (5 pre-existing serve.ts errors tracked under R015-T6 remain). bun build src/main.tsx browser-target: clean (1672 modules, 7.3MB). Ran bun install — @tauri-apps/api was missing from node_modules despite being in package.json; lockfile updated.

Live wire-up adapted to R024-T2 contract. Every arch_* command now requires rig_id; updated env/types.ts (added WireRigDto + WireRigKind), env/index.ts Rpc interface (rigList(); listTickets/listRelays/getTicket all take rigId: string first), env/tauri.ts (invoke('rig_list'), threads rigId in invoke args; browser.ts ignores rigId stub args). App.tsx now: queries env.rpc.rigList() on mount, picks last-active rig (sort by lastActiveAt desc), passes its id to listTickets/listRelays. event_bridge wraps payload as { rig_id, ...event }, so onEvent handler casts and filters index_finished by rig_id. bun run typecheck: clean (5 pre-existing serve.ts errors remain under R015-T6). bun build src/main.tsx --target=browser: clean (1672 modules, 7.31MB). cargo build -p yah-tauri: clean. End-to-end smoke unverified — needs YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri to actually launch the window and confirm Board shows R013/R014/R015/R016/R017/R018/R019/R022/R024 cards.

ArchView/hooks (R015-T5) will need the same rig_id threading: rpc.subgraph/lookup/node take rig_id under the new contract — that's blocked on R015-F1 hooks, not on this ticket

rig selector UI (R024-T3): replace mockRigs with a live state seeded from rig_list(); call rig_set_active and refetch when user picks; expose a setter so Board can re-target the active rig instead of capturing once on mount

Every arch_* command now requires rig_id; updated env/types.ts (added WireRigDto + WireRigKind), env/index.ts Rpc interface (rigList(); listTickets/listRelays/getTicket all take rigId: string first), env/tauri.ts (invoke('rig_list'), threads rigId in invoke args; browser.ts ignores rigId stub args). after: Board live wire-up shipped against the R024-T2 multi-rig contract. Wire types in env/types.ts (WireWorkItem*, ListTickets/Relays/GetTicket params+results, WireRigDto/WireRigKind). Rpc interface in env/index.ts: rigList() + listTickets/listRelays/getTicket each take rigId: string. env/tauri.ts invokes rig_list / arch_list_tickets / arch_list_relays / arch_get_ticket and threads rigId; env/browser.ts returns empty stubs. env/mapper.ts → workItemToTicket() (anchors[0] → file/line, narrows kind, defaults status='open', isZone only on explicit kind(epic)). App.tsx: rigList() on mount, picks last-active rig (sort by lastActiveAt desc), refetches via listTickets+listRelays, subscribes to wrapped { rig_id, ...event } and refilters index_finished by rig_id. mockTickets still seeds initial state so the UI renders without an attached rig. typecheck/browser bundle/cargo build all clean. End-to-end smoke unverified.

tauri-plugin-store 2.x ships with @tauri-apps/plugin-store@2.4.2 — both pulled fresh in this pass; if the workspace pins to a different tauri minor, check the plugin's Tauri compat matrix.
Browser snapshots survive across page reloads (localStorage), so manually clearing devtools storage is the only way to invalidate stale cache during dev — the LRU only evicts on writes.

KVStore wrapper landed end-to-end. types.ts: KVStore interface (get/set/remove/keys, all async, T|null). env/index.ts: Env extended with kv; getEnv() lazy-builds tauri kv via makeKv() and uses static export from browser. env/browser.ts: localStorage-backed kv namespaced under 'yah-ui:' with safeStorage() guarding incognito/sandboxed throws. env/tauri.ts: LazyStore('yah-ui-kv.json', {defaults:{}, autoSave:250}) wrapped to normalize undefined→null on get. Rust side: tauri-plugin-store=2 in Cargo.toml, .plugin(tauri_plugin_store::Builder::default().build()) in lib.rs run(), 'store:default' in capabilities/default.json. useArchGraph: snapshots persisted under 'subgraph:v1:{rigId}|{root}|{depth}' with {ts, data}; LRU cap of 8 entries enforced after each set. Cold-boot path: in-memory cache → kv.get() in parallel with rpc.subgraph(); kv hit warms UI (loading=true) but live fetch always wins final state and re-persists.

before: Extend Env interface with kv: KVStore alongside rpc
Implement in browser.ts via window.localStorage and tauri.ts via @tauri-apps/plugin-store
Persist last-N (rigId, root, depth) subgraph snapshots so cold-boot has something to render before first IndexFinished
after: Extend Env interface with kv: KVStore alongside rpc
Implement in browser.ts via window.localStorage and tauri.ts via @tauri-apps/plugin-store
Persist last-N (rigId, root, depth) subgraph snapshots so cold-boot has something to render before first IndexFinished
Smoke under Tauri: launch yah-tauri, render an arch view, kill it, relaunch — the same (rigId, root, depth) view should render the cached subgraph before index_finished arrives.
Consider plumbing kv into Board's listTickets/listRelays so cold-boot board renders before the daemon's first scan finishes (mirrors the subgraph hydrate).
cd yah-ui && bun run typecheck
cd yah-ui && bun build src/main.tsx --outdir /tmp/_yahui_kv_check --target=browser
cargo build -p yah-tauri

ArchEvent stream is the right truth-source for 'connection healthy'. If a future SSE-only error path (e.g. transport disconnect with no events) needs to flip the dot independently of t_last_index, this hook needs an explicit error channel from env.rpc.onEvent.
useConnectionStatus uses a 1s interval to re-derive state from t_last_index — fine for a single instance under TitleBar, but don't blanket the tree with this hook (each instance ticks independently).

ConnectionStrip landed end-to-end. New file yah-ui/src/components/shell/ConnectionStrip.tsx hosts the @yah ticket and renders a slim chrome bar (h-7 border-b under TitleBar) that returns null on 'live' and slides in for 'stale'/'offline'. New useConnectionStatus() hook in yah-ui/src/env/hooks.ts stamps t_last_index from useArchEvents(_, 'index_finished'); a 1s setInterval re-derives age so the badge demotes Live→Stale→Offline without waiting on a fresh event. Thresholds match the ticket: <5s live, 5-30s stale, >=30s or null offline. State dot uses --color-brass for stale and --color-oxblood for offline; copy reads 'last sync 12s ago' (or 'rig unreachable — no index events received yet' on null). App.tsx mounts <ConnectionStrip /> between TitleBar and TabStrip. Ticket annotation moved off ArchView.tsx onto the new component file. typecheck clean (only pre-existing serve.ts errors under R015-T6); bun build src/main.tsx --target=browser clean (1675 modules, 7.32MB).

before: Track t_last_index in a hook listening on arch:event index_finished
Live <5s, Stale 5-30s, Offline >30s or no events ever
after: Track t_last_index in a hook listening on arch:event index_finished
Live <5s, Stale 5-30s, Offline >30s or no events ever

End-to-end smoke under Tauri: YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri — strip should render 'offline' on cold boot, flip to 'live' after first index_finished, demote to 'stale' after 5s of idle, return to 'offline' after 30s.

Browser dev (bun run dev): browser env adapter never emits index_finished, so strip stays in 'offline'. Confirm copy reads 'rig unreachable — no index events received yet' and the strip doesn't crowd the layout.
Consider piping a manual reindex retry button into the offline state — design doc shows '[retry]' affordance at architecture/yah-ui-implementation-guide.md:377.
cd yah-ui && bun run typecheck
cd yah-ui && bun build src/main.tsx --outdir /tmp/_yahui_R015_F4 --target=browser

before: Depends on R015's hook tickets landing first
cd yah-ui && bun run dev  # Architecture tab shows real KG when daemon is running
after: cd yah-ui && bun run typecheck

rig_id captured by useArchGraph effect re-fires when App.tsx setRigId triggers a remount of the dependent path (key includes rigId, so cache invalidates). Rig-switch UX is exercised through R024-T3's selector — confirmed by the existing rigSetActive flow

Layer derivation is just qualified-name's leading segment (e.g. yah_kg::store::index → yah_kg). When @arch:layer(...) annotations land server-side, deriveLayer() in env/mapper.ts is the consolidation point — until then layer hues are coarse

Edge-kind collapse drops 'koda' edges entirely (placeholder type, intentional). All other wire EdgeKinds map into the six visual buckets — adding a new wire kind requires extending mapEdgeKind() or it'll be a TS exhaustiveness error

useArchGraph subscribes to ALL arch:event payloads regardless of rig_id today; in a multi-rig setup the event_bridge wraps payloads with rig_id but useArchGraph doesn't filter — fine for single-active-rig but wrong if two ArchViews mount for different rigs

ArchView swapped from mock.ts to live env-adapter data via useArchGraph. Wire→local subgraph mapper in env/mapper.ts (subgraphToArchSubgraph): wire NodeRef → ArchNode (label→shortName, qualified-head→layer fallback, span.start_line→line, doc deferred to useNode); wire EdgeOut.kind tagged-union collapsed to the six visual buckets GraphPane filters on (calls/imports/refs/macro/derived/attributed/bounds/extends/decorated_by → depends_on; impl_of_trait/conforms_to → implements; contains/defines/re_exports/refers_to → context; flow → message_flow; tag → data_flow; koda dropped). Edges with endpoints outside the returned node set are dropped (mermaid breaks on dangles). ArchView now takes rigId prop, calls useArchGraph(rigId, rootId, depth), maps to ArchSubgraph in a memo, and renders splash for {error / loading-with-no-cache / empty}; the empty splash flips caption based on loading. App.tsx threads rigId through TabPane and into ArchView. archRoot seed is now empty-string so first paint shows the splash instead of an arbitrary mock id. Rpc surface threaded rigId through every arch_* method (subgraph/lookup/node/neighbors/roots/stats/languages/openRig/closeRig/reindexPath/touch); tauri.ts invokes mirror, browser.ts stubs accept the new arg. useNode and useLookup also take rigId so cross-tab nav can resolve. typecheck clean (serve.ts Bun-globals errors are the pre-existing R015-T6 cleanup); browser bundle clean. End-to-end smoke unverified — needs YAH_RIG_ROOT=… cargo run -p yah-tauri.

End-to-end smoke: YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri — open the Architecture tab, paste a real 32-char NodeId from arch.list_relays/tickets results into the Root field, expect a populated graph (not the splash) and live updates on file edits within the rig

jumpToFile (App.tsx) still uses the basename-stem heuristic; with live data this never matches a real NodeId. Replace with an env().rpc.lookup(rigId, file, line) call that resolves the chip to a NodeId before setArchRoot — needs jumpToFile to become async (and a setTab on resolve). Tracking under R015-T5 follow-up or a dedicated cross-tab-nav ticket
RootSelector currently a free-text input — needs a roots() picker (rpc.roots) so the user can pick a real NodeId without copy-pasting hex
Inferred-epic surfacing: same gap as R015-T7 — relay-with-bare-R-children isn't flagged on the wire, so subgraph/Board don't surface mixed-children smell

ticket.title: useArchGraph + useNode + useLookup hooks over env().rpc
next_steps: useArchGraph(rigId, rootNodeId, depth): rpc.subgraph on mount + arch:event for nodes_added/changed/removed; cache by (rigId, root, depth)
useNode(id) and useLookup(file, line) for cross-tab navigation from path:line chips

ticket.title: useArchEvents(filter?) generic subscription helper
next_steps: Wraps env().rpc.onEvent with an optional ArchEvent kind filter and cleanup on unmount

ticket.title: KVStore wrapper: localStorage in browser, @tauri-apps/plugin-store in Tauri
handoff: KVStore wrapper landed end-to-end. types.ts: KVStore interface (get/set/remove/keys, all async, T|null). env/index.ts: Env extended with kv; getEnv() lazy-builds tauri kv via makeKv() and uses static export from browser. env/browser.ts: localStorage-backed kv namespaced under 'yah-ui:' with safeStorage() guarding incognito/sandboxed throws. env/tauri.ts: LazyStore('yah-ui-kv.json', {defaults:{}, autoSave:250}) wrapped to normalize undefined→null on get. Rust side: tauri-plugin-store=2 in Cargo.toml, .plugin(tauri_plugin_store::Builder::default().build()) in lib.rs run(), 'store:default' in capabilities/default.json. useArchGraph: snapshots persisted under 'subgraph:v1:{rigId}|{root}|{depth}' with {ts, data}; LRU cap of 8 entries enforced after each set. Cold-boot path: in-memory cache → kv.get() in parallel with rpc.subgraph(); kv hit warms UI (loading=true) but live fetch always wins final state and re-persists.
next_steps: Extend Env interface with kv: KVStore alongside rpc
Implement in browser.ts via window.localStorage and tauri.ts via @tauri-apps/plugin-store
Persist last-N (rigId, root, depth) subgraph snapshots so cold-boot has something to render before first IndexFinished
Smoke under Tauri: launch yah-tauri, render an arch view, kill it, relaunch — the same (rigId, root, depth) view should render the cached subgraph before index_finished arrives.
Consider plumbing kv into Board's listTickets/listRelays so cold-boot board renders before the daemon's first scan finishes (mirrors the subgraph hydrate).
gotchas: Browser snapshots survive across page reloads (localStorage), so manually clearing devtools storage is the only way to invalidate stale cache during dev — the LRU only evicts on writes.
assumes: tauri-plugin-store 2.x ships with @tauri-apps/plugin-store@2.4.2 — both pulled fresh in this pass; if the workspace pins to a different tauri minor, check the plugin's Tauri compat matrix.

ticket.title: ConnectionStrip: Live/Stale/Offline derived from time since last IndexFinished
handoff: ConnectionStrip landed end-to-end. New file yah-ui/src/components/shell/ConnectionStrip.tsx hosts the @yah ticket and renders a slim chrome bar (h-7 border-b under TitleBar) that returns null on 'live' and slides in for 'stale'/'offline'. New useConnectionStatus() hook in yah-ui/src/env/hooks.ts stamps t_last_index from useArchEvents(_, 'index_finished'); a 1s setInterval re-derives age so the badge demotes Live→Stale→Offline without waiting on a fresh event. Thresholds match the ticket: <5s live, 5-30s stale, >=30s or null offline. State dot uses --color-brass for stale and --color-oxblood for offline; copy reads 'last sync 12s ago' (or 'rig unreachable — no index events received yet' on null). App.tsx mounts <ConnectionStrip /> between TitleBar and TabStrip. Ticket annotation moved off ArchView.tsx onto the new component file. typecheck clean (only pre-existing serve.ts errors under R015-T6); bun build src/main.tsx --target=browser clean (1675 modules, 7.32MB).
next_steps: Track t_last_index in a hook listening on arch:event index_finished
Live <5s, Stale 5-30s, Offline >30s or no events ever

End-to-end smoke under Tauri: YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri — strip should render 'offline' on cold boot, flip to 'live' after first index_finished, demote to 'stale' after 5s of idle, return to 'offline' after 30s.

Browser dev (bun run dev): browser env adapter never emits index_finished, so strip stays in 'offline'. Confirm copy reads 'rig unreachable — no index events received yet' and the strip doesn't crowd the layout.
Consider piping a manual reindex retry button into the offline state — design doc shows '[retry]' affordance at architecture/yah-ui-implementation-guide.md:377.
gotchas: useConnectionStatus uses a 1s interval to re-derive state from t_last_index — fine for a single instance under TitleBar, but don't blanket the tree with this hook (each instance ticks independently).
assumes: ArchEvent stream is the right truth-source for 'connection healthy'. If a future SSE-only error path (e.g. transport disconnect with no events) needs to flip the dot independently of t_last_index, this hook needs an explicit error channel from env.rpc.onEvent.

ticket.title: yah-ui P3: Architecture live wiring + env-adapter hooks
useArchGraph + useNode + useLookup hooks over env().rpc
next_steps: useArchGraph(rigId, rootNodeId, depth): rpc.subgraph on mount + arch:event for nodes_added/changed/removed; cache by (rigId, root, depth)
useNode(id) and useLookup(file, line) for cross-tab navigation from path:line chips

sourceLines: //! @yah:relay(R015, "yah-ui P3: Architecture live wiring + env-adapter hooks")
//! @yah:status(in-progress)
//! @yah:phase(P3)
//! @yah:parent(R006)
//! @yah:ticket(R015-F1, "useArchGraph + useNode + useLookup hooks over env().rpc")
//! @yah:assignee(agent:claude)
//! @yah:status(review)
//! @yah:phase(P3)
//! @yah:parent(R015)
//! @yah:next("useArchGraph(rigId, rootNodeId, depth): rpc.subgraph on mount + arch:event for nodes_added/changed/removed; cache by (rigId, root, depth)")
//! @yah:next("useNode(id) and useLookup(file, line) for cross-tab navigation from path:line chips")
//! @yah:verify("cd yah-ui && bun run typecheck")
//! @yah:ticket(R015-F2, "useArchEvents(filter?) generic subscription helper")
//! @yah:assignee(agent:claude)
//! @yah:status(review)
//! @yah:phase(P3)
//! @yah:parent(R015)
//! @yah:next("Wraps env().rpc.onEvent with an optional ArchEvent kind filter and cleanup on unmount")
//! @yah:ticket(R015-F3, "KVStore wrapper: localStorage in browser, @tauri-apps/plugin-store in Tauri")
//! @yah:assignee(agent:claude)
//! @yah:status(review)
//! @yah:phase(P3)
//! @yah:parent(R015)
//! @yah:next("Extend Env interface with kv: KVStore alongside rpc")
//! @yah:next("Implement in browser.ts via window.localStorage and tauri.ts via @tauri-apps/plugin-store")
//! @yah:next("Persist last-N (rigId, root, depth) subgraph snapshots so cold-boot has something to render before first IndexFinished")

//! @yah:handoff("KVStore wrapper landed end-to-end. types.ts: KVStore interface (get/set/remove/keys, all async, T|null). env/index.ts: Env extended with kv; getEnv() lazy-builds tauri kv via makeKv() and uses static export from browser. env/browser.ts: localStorage-backed kv namespaced under 'yah-ui:' with safeStorage() guarding incognito/sandboxed throws. env/tauri.ts: LazyStore('yah-ui-kv.json', {defaults:{}, autoSave:250}) wrapped to normalize undefined→null on get. Rust side: tauri-plugin-store=2 in Cargo.toml, .plugin(tauri_plugin_store::Builder::default().build()) in lib.rs run(), 'store:default' in capabilities/default.json. useArchGraph: snapshots persisted under 'subgraph:v1:{rigId}|{root}|{depth}' with {ts, data}; LRU cap of 8 entries enforced after each set. Cold-boot path: in-memory cache → kv.get() in parallel with rpc.subgraph(); kv hit warms UI (loading=true) but live fetch always wins final state and re-persists.")
//! @yah:next("Smoke under Tauri: launch yah-tauri, render an arch view, kill it, relaunch — the same (rigId, root, depth) view should render the cached subgraph before index_finished arrives.")
//! @yah:next("Consider plumbing kv into Board's listTickets/listRelays so cold-boot board renders before the daemon's first scan finishes (mirrors the subgraph hydrate).")
//! @yah:verify("cd yah-ui && bun run typecheck")
//! @yah:verify("cd yah-ui && bun build src/main.tsx --outdir /tmp/_yahui_kv_check --target=browser")
//! @yah:verify("cargo build -p yah-tauri")

//! @yah:gotcha("Browser snapshots survive across page reloads (localStorage), so manually clearing devtools storage is the only way to invalidate stale cache during dev — the LRU only evicts on writes.")

//! @yah:assumes("tauri-plugin-store 2.x ships with @tauri-apps/plugin-store@2.4.2 — both pulled fresh in this pass; if the workspace pins to a different tauri minor, check the plugin's Tauri compat matrix.")
//! @yah:ticket(R019-F3, "Frontend env adapter SSH backend: third backend alongside tauri/browser")
//! @yah:status(open)
//! @yah:phase(P3)
//! @yah:parent(R019)
//! @yah:next("When a remote rig is selected, route rpc calls over a Tauri command that fronts yah-rpc-ssh")
//! @yah:next("Connection state surfaces in the existing ConnectionStrip (R015)")
//! @yah:ticket(R015-T7, "Wire Board tab to live env adapter: listTickets/listRelays/getTicket + WorkItem types + replace mockTickets")
//! @yah:status(review)
//! @yah:assignee(agent:claude)
//! @yah:phase(P3)
//! @yah:parent(R015)

//! @yah:handoff("Board live wire-up shipped against the R024-T2 multi-rig contract. Wire types in env/types.ts (WireWorkItem*, ListTickets/Relays/GetTicket params+results, WireRigDto/WireRigKind). Rpc interface in env/index.ts: rigList() + listTickets/listRelays/getTicket each take rigId: string. env/tauri.ts invokes rig_list / arch_list_tickets / arch_list_relays / arch_get_ticket and threads rigId; env/browser.ts returns empty stubs. env/mapper.ts → workItemToTicket() (anchors[0] → file/line, narrows kind, defaults status='open', isZone only on explicit kind(epic)). App.tsx: rigList() on mount, picks last-active rig (sort by lastActiveAt desc), refetches via listTickets+listRelays, subscribes to wrapped { rig_id, ...event } and refilters index_finished by rig_id. mockTickets still seeds initial state so the UI renders without an attached rig. typecheck/browser bundle/cargo build all clean. End-to-end smoke unverified.")

//! @yah:next("End-to-end smoke: YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri — Board should show live R013/R014/R015/R016/R017/R018/R019/R022/R024 cards (not mock R012 set) and re-render on file edits within the rig")

//! @yah:next("Inferred-epic surfacing (relay with bare-R children): WorkItem doesn't carry an isZone-equivalent; either compute server-side in arch.list_relays, or post-process in workItemToTicket using the parent map")
//! @yah:next("ArchView/hooks (R015-T5/F1) will need the same rig_id threading: rpc.subgraph/lookup/node take rig_id under the new contract — out of scope here")

//! @yah:next("Rig selector UI (R024-T3): replace mockRigs with state seeded from rig_list(); call rig_set_active and refetch on pick; expose a setter so Board re-targets instead of capturing rigId once on mount")
//! @yah:verify("cd yah-ui && bun run typecheck")
//! @yah:verify("cd yah-ui && bun build src/main.tsx --outdir /tmp/_yahui_build_check --target=browser")
//! @yah:verify("cargo build -p yah-tauri")
//! @yah:gotcha("App.tsx still falls back to mockTickets when env is browser OR rigList()/refetch returns empty — keep dev-without-Tauri working")
//! @yah:gotcha("childCounts on zone cards is currently undefined — TitleBar/Board don't render the smell badge for live data until inferred-epic surfacing lands")
//! @yah:gotcha("targetRigId is captured once on mount; switching rigs at runtime needs the R024-T3 selector to feed it back in (or a useEffect dep on active rig)")

//! @yah:assumes("rig_list returns an entry whenever YAH_RIG_ROOT is set — boot_registry in app/tauri/src/lib.rs auto-attaches that path before the renderer mounts; if there's a race, the first refetch sees [] and falls back to mockTickets until index_finished triggers a retry")
//! @yah:cleanup("serve.ts Bun-globals errors are pre-existing — handled under R015-T6")

lastTicket.title: Wire Board tab to live env adapter: listTickets/listRelays/getTicket + WorkItem types + replace mockTickets
next_steps: End-to-end smoke: YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri — Board should show live R013/R014/R015/R016/R017/R018/R019/R022/R024 cards (not mock R012 set) and re-render on file edits within the rig

Inferred-epic surfacing (relay with bare-R children): WorkItem doesn't carry an isZone-equivalent; either compute server-side in arch.list_relays, or post-process in workItemToTicket using the parent map
ArchView/hooks (R015-T5/F1) will need the same rig_id threading: rpc.subgraph/lookup/node take rig_id under the new contract — out of scope here
Rig selector UI (R024-T3): replace mockRigs with state seeded from rig_list(); call rig_set_active and refetch on pick; expose a setter so Board re-targets instead of capturing rigId once on mount
gotchas: App.tsx still falls back to mockTickets when env is browser OR rigList()/refetch returns empty — keep dev-without-Tauri working
childCounts on zone cards is currently undefined — TitleBar/Board don't render the smell badge for live data until inferred-epic surfacing lands
targetRigId is captured once on mount; switching rigs at runtime needs the R024-T3 selector to feed it back in (or a useEffect dep on active rig)
assumes: rig_list returns an entry whenever YAH_RIG_ROOT is set — boot_registry in app/tauri/src/lib.rs auto-attaches that path before the renderer mounts; if there's a race, the first refetch sees [] and falls back to mockTickets until index_finished triggers a retry
cleanup: serve.ts Bun-globals errors are pre-existing — handled under R015-T6

Rpc interface in env/index.ts: rigList() + listTickets/listRelays/getTicket each take rigId: string. env/tauri.ts invokes rig_list / arch_list_tickets / arch_list_relays / arch_get_ticket and threads rigId; env/browser.ts returns empty stubs. env/mapper.ts → workItemToTicket() (anchors[0] → file/line, narrows kind, defaults status='open', isZone only on explicit kind(epic)). App.tsx: rigList() on mount, picks last-active rig (sort by lastActiveAt desc), refetches via listTickets+listRelays, subscribes to wrapped { rig_id, ...event } and refilters index_finished by rig_id. mockTickets still seeds initial state so the UI renders without an attached rig. typecheck/browser bundle/cargo build all clean. End-to-end smoke unverified.

ArchView now takes rigId prop, calls useArchGraph(rigId, rootId, depth), maps to ArchSubgraph in a memo, and renders splash for {error / loading-with-no-cache / empty}; the empty splash flips caption based on loading. App.tsx threads rigId through TabPane and into ArchView. archRoot seed is now empty-string so first paint shows the splash instead of an arbitrary mock id. Rpc surface threaded rigId through every arch_* method (subgraph/lookup/node/neighbors/roots/stats/languages/openRig/closeRig/reindexPath/touch); tauri.ts invokes mirror, browser.ts stubs accept the new arg. useNode and useLookup also take rigId so cross-tab nav can resolve. typecheck clean (serve.ts Bun-globals errors are the pre-existing R015-T6 cleanup); browser bundle clean. End-to-end smoke unverified — needs YAH_RIG_ROOT=… cargo run -p yah-tauri.
next_steps: End-to-end smoke: YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri — open the Architecture tab, paste a real 32-char NodeId from arch.list_relays/tickets results into the Root field, expect a populated graph (not the splash) and live updates on file edits within the rig

jumpToFile (App.tsx) still uses the basename-stem heuristic; with live data this never matches a real NodeId. Replace with an env().rpc.lookup(rigId, file, line) call that resolves the chip to a NodeId before setArchRoot — needs jumpToFile to become async (and a setTab on resolve). Tracking under R015-T5 follow-up or a dedicated cross-tab-nav ticket
RootSelector currently a free-text input — needs a roots() picker (rpc.roots) so the user can pick a real NodeId without copy-pasting hex
Inferred-epic surfacing: same gap as R015-T7 — relay-with-bare-R-children isn't flagged on the wire, so subgraph/Board don't surface mixed-children smell
gotchas: Layer derivation is just qualified-name's leading segment (e.g. yah_kg::store::index → yah_kg). When @arch:layer(...) annotations land server-side, deriveLayer() in env/mapper.ts is the consolidation point — until then layer hues are coarse

Edge-kind collapse drops 'koda' edges entirely (placeholder type, intentional). All other wire EdgeKinds map into the six visual buckets — adding a new wire kind requires extending mapEdgeKind() or it'll be a TS exhaustiveness error

useArchGraph subscribes to ALL arch:event payloads regardless of rig_id today; in a multi-rig setup the event_bridge wraps payloads with rig_id but useArchGraph doesn't filter — fine for single-active-rig but wrong if two ArchViews mount for different rigs
assumes: rig_id captured by useArchGraph effect re-fires when App.tsx setRigId triggers a remount of the dependent path (key includes rigId, so cache invalidates). Rig-switch UX is exercised through R024-T3's selector — confirmed by the existing rigSetActive flow

sourceLines: //! @yah:ticket(R015-T5, "Swap ArchView from mock.ts to live env-adapter data via useArchGraph")
//! @yah:assignee(agent:claude)
//! @yah:status(review)
//! @yah:phase(P3)
//! @yah:parent(R015)
//! @yah:verify("cd yah-ui && bun run typecheck")

//! @yah:handoff("ArchView swapped from mock.ts to live env-adapter data via useArchGraph. Wire→local subgraph mapper in env/mapper.ts (subgraphToArchSubgraph): wire NodeRef → ArchNode (label→shortName, qualified-head→layer fallback, span.start_line→line, doc deferred to useNode); wire EdgeOut.kind tagged-union collapsed to the six visual buckets GraphPane filters on (calls/imports/refs/macro/derived/attributed/bounds/extends/decorated_by → depends_on; impl_of_trait/conforms_to → implements; contains/defines/re_exports/refers_to → context; flow → message_flow; tag → data_flow; koda dropped). Edges with endpoints outside the returned node set are dropped (mermaid breaks on dangles). ArchView now takes rigId prop, calls useArchGraph(rigId, rootId, depth), maps to ArchSubgraph in a memo, and renders splash for {error / loading-with-no-cache / empty}; the empty splash flips caption based on loading. App.tsx threads rigId through TabPane and into ArchView. archRoot seed is now empty-string so first paint shows the splash instead of an arbitrary mock id. Rpc surface threaded rigId through every arch_* method (subgraph/lookup/node/neighbors/roots/stats/languages/openRig/closeRig/reindexPath/touch); tauri.ts invokes mirror, browser.ts stubs accept the new arg. useNode and useLookup also take rigId so cross-tab nav can resolve. typecheck clean (serve.ts Bun-globals errors are the pre-existing R015-T6 cleanup); browser bundle clean. End-to-end smoke unverified — needs YAH_RIG_ROOT=… cargo run -p yah-tauri.")

//! @yah:next("End-to-end smoke: YAH_RIG_ROOT=/Users/leif/ss/rs-hack cargo run -p yah-tauri — open the Architecture tab, paste a real 32-char NodeId from arch.list_relays/tickets results into the Root field, expect a populated graph (not the splash) and live updates on file edits within the rig")

//! @yah:next("jumpToFile (App.tsx) still uses the basename-stem heuristic; with live data this never matches a real NodeId. Replace with an env().rpc.lookup(rigId, file, line) call that resolves the chip to a NodeId before setArchRoot — needs jumpToFile to become async (and a setTab on resolve). Tracking under R015-T5 follow-up or a dedicated cross-tab-nav ticket")
//! @yah:next("RootSelector currently a free-text input — needs a roots() picker (rpc.roots) so the user can pick a real NodeId without copy-pasting hex")
//! @yah:next("Inferred-epic surfacing: same gap as R015-T7 — relay-with-bare-R-children isn't flagged on the wire, so subgraph/Board don't surface mixed-children smell")

//! @yah:gotcha("Layer derivation is just qualified-name's leading segment (e.g. yah_kg::store::index → yah_kg). When @arch:layer(...) annotations land server-side, deriveLayer() in env/mapper.ts is the consolidation point — until then layer hues are coarse")

//! @yah:gotcha("Edge-kind collapse drops 'koda' edges entirely (placeholder type, intentional). All other wire EdgeKinds map into the six visual buckets — adding a new wire kind requires extending mapEdgeKind() or it'll be a TS exhaustiveness error")

//! @yah:gotcha("useArchGraph subscribes to ALL arch:event payloads regardless of rig_id today; in a multi-rig setup the event_bridge wraps payloads with rig_id but useArchGraph doesn't filter — fine for single-active-rig but wrong if two ArchViews mount for different rigs")

//! @yah:assumes("rig_id captured by useArchGraph effect re-fires when App.tsx setRigId triggers a remount of the dependent path (key includes rigId, so cache invalidates). Rig-switch UX is exercised through R024-T3's selector — confirmed by the existing rigSetActive flow")

ticket.title: useArchGraph + useNode + useLookup hooks over env().rpc
next_steps: useArchGraph(rigId, rootNodeId, depth): rpc.subgraph on mount + arch:event for nodes_added/changed/removed; cache by (rigId, root, depth)
useNode(id) and useLookup(file, line) for cross-tab navigation from path:line chips

ticket.title: useArchEvents(filter?) generic subscription helper
next_steps: Wraps env().rpc.onEvent with an optional ArchEvent kind filter and cleanup on unmount

ticket.title: KVStore wrapper: localStorage in browser, @tauri-apps/plugin-store in Tauri
next_steps: Extend Env interface with kv: KVStore alongside rpc
Implement in browser.ts via window.localStorage and tauri.ts via @tauri-apps/plugin-store
Persist last-N (rigId, root, depth) subgraph snapshots so cold-boot has something to render before first IndexFinished

ticket.title: Swap ArchView from mock.ts to live env-adapter data via useArchGraph
next_steps: Depends on R015's hook tickets landing first
verify: cd yah-ui && bun run dev  # Architecture tab shows real KG when daemon is running

ticket.title: Wire Board tab to live env adapter: listTickets/listRelays/getTicket + WorkItem types + replace mockTickets
next_steps: Add WorkItem/WorkItemAnno/TicketStatus/WorkItemAnchor/ListTicketsResult/ListRelaysResult/GetTicketResult types to yah-ui/src/env/types.ts
Extend Rpc interface in yah-ui/src/env/index.ts with listTickets/listRelays/getTicket
Wire invoke('arch_list_tickets'|'arch_list_relays'|'arch_get_ticket') in yah-ui/src/env/tauri.ts
Browser stub returns empty arrays in yah-ui/src/env/browser.ts
Add workItemToTicket() mapper (RPC WorkItem -> UI Ticket) — anchors[0] sources file/line; flatten anno.* fields
App.tsx: replace useState(mockTickets) with effect that getEnv() + listTickets + listRelays, plus arch:event index_finished re-fetch
gotchas: App.tsx still falls back to mockTickets when env is browser OR no active rig — keep dev-without-Tauri working
assumes: R024-T2 will land arch_attach_rig/arch_set_active_rig commands so the user can pick a rig from the UI; until then YAH_RIG_ROOT is the bootstrap path
